i want you to plan an extremely detailed plan to add a feature to the portal for all users where theres a tab to see where crews are in real time. the crew should be able to login to the portal and mark themselves as active. we should be able to ask to use their location and then it will put them  on   a map that shows where different crews are. when you hover over the crew dot on the map the admin is the only one who should be able to see the info about the crew when hovering. every other user should only be able to see the crew first name. i want this to be next level so we need to get this right. use your full capability to plan this. feel free to add things to this feature to take it to the next level 

repo amgaviation/amg1

i want you to plan an extremely detailed plan to add a feature to the portal for all users where theres a tab to see where crews are in real time. the crew should be able to login to the portal and mark themselves as active. we should be able to ask to use their location and then it will put them  on   a map that shows where different crews are. when you hover over the crew dot on the map the admin is the only one who should be able to see the info about the crew when hovering. every other user should only be able to see the crew first name. i want this to be next level so we need to get this right. use your full capability to plan this. feel free to add things to this feature to take it to the next level 

repo amgaviation/amg1

questions.question: What's the frontend stack of the amg1 portal? (I can see your Supabase backend but not the repo code.)

options.label: Next.js (React)
options.description: App Router or Pages Router — most common with Supabase

options.label: React + Vite (SPA)
options.description: Client-side React app built with Vite

options.label: Other / not sure
options.description: I'll write the plan framework-agnostic with React as the reference implementation

questions.question: Which map provider should the live crew map use?

options.label: Mapbox GL (Recommended)
options.description: Best-looking, smooth animations, clustering, dark aviation-style themes. Free up to 50k map loads/month, then paid.

options.label: Google Maps
options.description: Familiar look, great geocoding. Requires billing account; $200/mo free credit.

options.label: Leaflet + OpenStreetMap
options.description: Fully free and open source. Less polished animations but zero cost and no API keys.

questions.question: How should crew location tracking behave?

options.label: While portal open (Recommended)
options.description: Browser geolocation updates while the crew has the portal open (works as installed PWA too). Simple, privacy-friendly, no app store.

options.label: Check-in pings only
options.description: Crew taps 'I'm active' and location is captured at that moment + periodic manual refreshes. Lowest battery/privacy impact.

options.label: Plan for background tracking
options.description: Include a phase for a companion mobile app (Capacitor/React Native) for true background tracking — bigger scope.

options.description: Familiar look, great geocoding. Requires billing account; $200/mo free credit.

options.label: Leaflet + OpenStreetMap
options.description: Fully free and open source. Less polished animations but zero cost and no API keys.

questions.question: How should crew location tracking behave?

options.label: While portal open (Recommended)
options.description: Browser geolocation updates while the crew has the portal open (works as installed PWA too). Simple, privacy-friendly, no app store.

options.label: Check-in pings only
options.description: Crew taps 'I'm active' and location is captured at that moment + periodic manual refreshes. Lowest battery/privacy impact.

options.label: Plan for background tracking
options.description: Include a phase for a companion mobile app (Capacitor/React Native) for true background tracking — bigger scope.

# AMG Aviation Portal — Live Crew Map ("AMG LiveOps")
## Implementation Plan v1.0 — July 7, 2026

---

## 1. Executive summary

Add a **Live Ops** tab to the portal where every logged-in user sees a real-time map of active crews. Crew members opt in ("Go Active"), grant browser location permission, and appear as a live dot. Hovering a dot shows **full crew intel for admins only**; every other role sees **first name only**. The feature is built on the stack you already run: Wix Velo pages/backend for auth and UI, **Supabase (Realtime + Postgres/PostGIS)** as the location engine, and **Mapbox GL JS** inside an HTML embed for the map itself.

Phases:
- **Phase 1 (MVP, ~1.5–2 wks):** Active toggle, location capture while portal open, live map, role-based hover, stale/offline handling.
- **Phase 2 (Ops-grade, ~1–2 wks):** Mission overlay, geofenced airport auto-check-in, history trails, alerts, admin roster panel.
- **Phase 3 (Background tracking):** Capacitor-wrapped companion app for true background location on iOS/Android.

---

## 2. What was reviewed to build this plan

**Repo `amgaviation/amg1` (Wix Velo Git integration):**
- `src/backend/portalAuth.jsw` — role/status routing (`amg_admin`, `crew_pilot`, `client_owner`, `maintenance_partner`, `broker_partner`; statuses pending/approved/denied/suspended), audit logging to `PortalAuditLogs`, `publicUser()` shaping pattern.
- `src/backend/crewProfiles.jsw` — `CrewProfiles` collection field map, `getMyCrewDashboard`/`saveMyCrewProfile` pattern.
- `src/pages/*` — Crew/Admin/Client/Partner dashboards, `Portal Router` role redirect, `masterPage.js`.
- `src/public/Site.js` — existing postMessage handler for HTML-embed resizing (`wix-amg-resize`) — the exact pattern the map embed will extend.
- `src/backend/permissions.json` — currently `*` invokable by `anonymous` (see §10 security flags).

**Supabase project `AMG Aviation Portal` (vsynqnqlouvphiniqaiy, us-east-2, PG 17):**
- 76 public tables incl. `profiles` (role: super_admin/admin/crew/client/partner), `crew_profiles` (70 cols), `missions`, `mission_crew_assignments`, `crew_availability`, `notifications`, `audit_events`, `consent_events`, `role_permissions` (per-module can_view/add/edit/delete matrix).
- Extensions available: **PostGIS** (not yet installed), **pg_cron** (installed), Realtime enabled at platform level. No edge functions deployed yet.

---

## 3. Requirements

### 3.1 User stories
| # | As a… | I want… | So that… |
|---|-------|---------|----------|
| U1 | Crew member | to toggle "Go Active" and share my location | dispatch knows I'm available and where I am |
| U2 | Crew member | to stop sharing instantly, and have sharing auto-expire | I control my privacy |
| U3 | Admin | to see every active crew on a live map with full details on hover | I can assign the closest qualified crew to a mission |
| U4 | Admin | to see stale/offline crews visually distinguished | I don't rely on dead data |
| U5 | Any portal user | to see active crews as first-name-only dots | I get situational awareness without exposing crew PII |
| U6 | Admin | to overlay live missions (dep/arr airports, assigned crew) | one screen for the whole operation |
| U7 | Compliance | consent + every location event auditable, data auto-purged | we meet privacy obligations |

### 3.2 Visibility matrix (enforced server-side, never in the browser)
| Data on hover/panel | super_admin / amg_admin | crew_pilot | client_owner | partners |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| First name | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Full name, phone, email | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Precise coordinates | ✅ | ❌ (fuzzed ~1 km) | ❌ (fuzzed) | ❌ (fuzzed) |
| Crew status, base airport, certificates, type ratings, day rate | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Current mission ref + role | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Last-update time, active/stale state | ✅ | ✅ (coarse: "active/stale") | ✅ | ✅ |
| History trail (24 h) | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |

Key rule: **non-admin clients never receive redacted fields at all** — redaction happens in the backend/RLS layer, so "view source" or the network tab reveals nothing.

### 3.3 Non-functional
- Dot movement latency ≤ 2 s admin view (Realtime push); ≤ 20 s acceptable fallback (polling).
- Battery-sane: adaptive ping interval (15 s moving / 60 s stationary), `enableHighAccuracy` only when moving.
- Stale = no ping for 3 min (amber); auto-deactivate at 30 min; hard session cap 16 h (duty-day-shaped).
- Map renders 200+ dots at 60 fps (clustering beyond 50).
- Works on mobile Safari/Chrome; the crew flow is thumb-friendly.

---

## 4. Architecture

### 4.1 Recommended: Wix = auth/UI, Supabase = location engine, Mapbox in HTML embed

```
┌─ Crew phone/laptop (Wix page: Live Ops) ─────────────────────┐
│  Velo page code ── postMessage ──► HTML embed (tracker+map) │
│   • Go Active toggle              • navigator.geolocation    │
│   • role/session bootstrap        •  watchPosition()         │
└──────────────┬───────────────────────────┬──────────────────┘
               │ (1) goActive/goInactive   │ (3) location pings (HTTPS)
               ▼                           ▼
     Wix Velo backend  locationTracking.jsw  ◄─ verifies member, role,
               │  service-role key from wix-secrets-backend     consent, rate limits
               ▼
     Supabase Postgres:  crew_live_status / crew_location_pings (PostGIS)
               │ triggers publish
               ▼
     Supabase Realtime broadcast:  crew-map:admin  |  crew-map:public
               │ (4) WebSocket, short-lived role-scoped JWT minted by Wix backend
               ▼
┌─ Every viewer's browser (same Wix page) ─────────────────────┐
│  HTML embed: Mapbox GL JS map                                │
│   admins subscribe to :admin (full payload)                  │
│   others subscribe to :public (first_name + fuzzed coords)   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

Why this split:
- **Velo page code runs in a worker** — no `navigator.geolocation.watchPosition`. `wixWindow.getCurrentGeolocation()` exists but is one-shot. The HTML embed is a real iframe with full browser APIs → continuous watch, WebSockets, WebGL (Mapbox). Your `Site.js` already speaks postMessage to embeds, so this matches house style.
- **Supabase gives you** sub-second Realtime push, PostGIS distance queries ("nearest qualified crew to KTEB"), pg_cron retention purges, and RLS-authorized channels. You already pay for/maintain this project.
- **Wix backend stays the only writer** — it holds the service-role key in Wix Secrets, verifies `currentMember` + role + consent on every call, shapes payloads, and mints viewer JWTs. Browsers never hold privileged credentials.

### 4.2 Fallback option (pure Wix, no Supabase)
Store pings in a `CrewLiveLocations` Wix collection; viewers poll a backend method every 15–20 s that returns a role-shaped payload. Simpler (no second system), but: no push (15–20 s latency), no PostGIS, retention via scheduled jobs, and heavier wix-data read quotas with many viewers. Keep as fallback; not recommended for "next level."

### 4.3 Realtime channel design
- `crew-map:admin` — full payloads. Channel authorization via RLS on `realtime.messages`, requiring JWT claim `portal_role in ('admin','super_admin')`.
- `crew-map:public` — `{crew_id, first_name, lat_fuzzed, lng_fuzzed, state}` only.
- JWTs: minted by `locationTracking.jsw` (HS256 with Supabase JWT secret from Wix Secrets), 15-min expiry, embed refreshes via postMessage → page code → backend.
- Fuzzing: deterministic per-crew-per-hour offset (≤ ~1 km) so dots don't jitter but can't be de-fuzzed by averaging.

---

## 5. Data model (Supabase migrations)

```sql
-- 001: enable PostGIS
create extension if not exists postgis;

-- 002: live status (one row per crew; the "current dot")
create table public.crew_live_status (
  crew_id          uuid primary key references public.profiles(id) on delete cascade,
  is_active        boolean not null default false,
  activated_at     timestamptz,
  deactivated_at   timestamptz,
  deactivation_reason text,            -- manual | timeout | duty_cap | admin
  last_ping_at     timestamptz,
  geom             geography(point, 4326),
  lat              double precision,
  lng              double precision,
  accuracy_m       double precision,
  speed_mps        double precision,
  heading_deg      double precision,
  battery_pct      integer,
  activity_state   text default 'stationary',  -- moving | stationary
  status_note      text,               -- crew-entered, e.g. "At FBO, wheels up 1400Z"
  duty_status      text default 'available',   -- available | on_mission | resting
  mission_id       uuid references public.missions(id),
  device_info      jsonb,
  consent_version  text,
  updated_at       timestamptz not null default now()
);
create index on public.crew_live_status using gist (geom);
create index on public.crew_live_status (is_active, last_ping_at);

-- 003: ping history (trails, replay, audit) — partitioned by day, purged by pg_cron
create table public.crew_location_pings (
  id           bigint generated always as identity,
  crew_id      uuid not null references public.profiles(id) on delete cascade,
  ping_at      timestamptz not null default now(),
  geom         geography(point, 4326) not null,
  accuracy_m   double precision,
  speed_mps    double precision,
  heading_deg  double precision,
  source       text not null default 'web',   -- web | companion_app
  primary key (id, ping_at)
) partition by range (ping_at);
create index on public.crew_location_pings (crew_id, ping_at desc);

-- 004: consent + audit
create table public.location_consent_events (
  id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  crew_id uuid not null references public.profiles(id),
  action text not null,          -- granted | revoked | auto_expired
  consent_version text not null,
  ip_hash text, user_agent text,
  created_at timestamptz not null default now()
);

-- 005: retention (pg_cron, already installed)
--   pings: 72 h;  fuzz cache: 24 h;  stale-sweep every minute
select cron.schedule('purge-pings','15 * * * *',
  $$delete from crew_location_pings where ping_at < now() - interval '72 hours'$$);
select cron.schedule('stale-sweep','* * * * *',
  $$update crew_live_status set is_active=false, deactivated_at=now(),
     deactivation_reason='timeout'
   where is_active and last_ping_at < now() - interval '30 minutes'$$);

-- 006: RLS — deny-all by default; only service role writes; viewers get data
-- exclusively via Realtime broadcast (authorized) and backend-shaped REST.
alter table crew_live_status enable row level security;
alter table crew_location_pings enable row level security;
alter table location_consent_events enable row level security;
-- realtime.messages policies gate crew-map:admin by jwt claim portal_role
```

---

## 6. Wix implementation

### 6.1 New/changed pages & navigation
- New page **“Live Ops” (`/live-ops`)**, added to portal nav for all approved roles. `Portal Router` untouched; page itself gates access.
- **Crew Dashboard**: add a compact “Location Sharing” card (Go Active toggle + status pill + note field) that deep-links to Live Ops. Crew can also toggle directly on Live Ops.
- **Admin Dashboard**: add “Live Ops” shortcut tile with active-crew count badge.

### 6.2 Wix Editor elements (Live Ops page)
| Element ID | Type | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `liveOpsEmbed` | HTML iframe embed (full-bleed, autoheight via existing `wix-amg-resize`) | Mapbox map + tracker runtime |
| `goActiveToggle` | Switch | crew only — visible per role |
| `statusPill` | Text | “ACTIVE — sharing”, “STALE”, “OFF” |
| `statusNoteInput` | TextInput | optional crew status note |
| `activeCountText` | Text | “7 crews active” |
| `rosterRepeater` | Repeater (admins only) | side list: name, duty status, last ping, ‘locate’ button → flyTo |
| `consentLightbox` | Lightbox | first-run consent copy + Accept/Decline |
| `mapMessage` | Text | errors: permission denied, offline, etc. |

### 6.3 New backend module: `src/backend/locationTracking.jsw`
Follows the `crewProfiles.jsw` conventions (`currentMember` verification, `publicX()` shapers, `logAction` audit).

```js
// exports (all verify member + approved status first, mirroring portalAuth.jsw)
export async function getLiveOpsBootstrap()   // role, crewId, mapbox public token,
                                              // supabase URL, role-scoped realtime JWT (15 min),
                                              // consent state, active session if any
export async function refreshRealtimeToken()  // re-mint JWT
export async function goActive(consentVersion, statusNote)   // upserts crew_live_status,
                                              // logs consent, publishes roster update
export async function goInactive(reason)      // manual stop
export async function postLocation(batch)     // 1–4 pings {lat,lng,acc,spd,hdg,batt,ts}
                                              // validates: is_active, rate ≤1 req/10s,
                                              // accuracy ≤ 150 m, teleport rejection (>300 kt),
                                              // then upserts status + inserts pings (Supabase REST,
                                              // service key from wix-secrets-backend)
export async function adminListLiveCrews()    // admin-only: full roster w/ profile join
export async function adminForceDeactivate(crewId)          // admin kill-switch, audited
export async function adminGetCrewTrail(crewId, hours)      // ≤24 h trail, admin-only
```

Secrets in **Wix Secrets Manager**: `SUPABASE_URL`, `SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY`, `SUPABASE_JWT_SECRET`, `MAPBOX_PUBLIC_TOKEN` (URL-restricted to your domains).

### 6.4 `permissions.json` — tighten while we’re here
Scope `locationTracking.jsw` (and ideally all portal modules) to `siteMember` only; admin functions still re-verify role in code:

```json
{ "web-methods": { "locationTracking.jsw": { "*": {
  "siteOwner": {"invoke": true}, "siteMember": {"invoke": true}, "anonymous": {"invoke": false}
}}}}
```

### 6.5 The HTML embed (`liveOpsEmbed`) — one self-contained file
Two responsibilities, one iframe (keeps postMessage plumbing minimal):

**A. Tracker runtime (crew only, activated by page code message):**
- `navigator.geolocation.watchPosition` with adaptive strategy: high-accuracy + 15 s cadence when speed > 1 m/s; low-power 60 s when stationary; pause when `document.hidden` > 5 min (send `paused` state).
- Client-side filter: drop fixes with accuracy > 150 m; dedupe fixes < 10 m apart when stationary; batch up to 4 fixes per `postLocation`.
- Battery API (where available) → `battery_pct`.
- Heartbeat: even when stationary, ping every 60 s so staleness is meaningful.
- All server calls go **postMessage → page code → backend `.jsw`** (embed never holds Wix session or service keys).

**B. Map viewer (all roles):**
- Mapbox GL JS v3, dark “ops” style (`navigation-night` or custom AMG-branded style), globe off, US-centered default with auto-fit to active dots.
- Realtime: `@supabase/supabase-js` in the iframe subscribes to `crew-map:admin` or `crew-map:public` per its JWT (server decides — the embed just uses the token it was given).
- **Dot rendering:** GeoJSON source + circle/symbol layers; pulsing halo on fresh pings (CSS/WebGL animation); color by state: green = active, amber = stale (>3 min), gray fading = went inactive < 10 min ago; heading arrow when moving; cluster layer beyond 50 dots with count badges.
- **Hover cards:** on `mouseenter` (tap on mobile):
  - Admin card: full name, photo/initials, phone (tap-to-call), duty status, mission ref + route, base airport, certs/type ratings chips, speed, last update (“12 s ago”), buttons: Locate / Trail / Message / Deactivate.
  - Everyone else: minimal chip — `● Tony — Active` and nothing more (payload literally contains nothing more).
- Controls: search/filter (admin), status filter chips, “fit all”, fullscreen, legend; roster panel sync (row click → `flyTo`).
- Resilience: WebSocket drop → auto-retry w/ backoff → fall back to 20 s polling via page code; banner shows degraded mode.

### 6.6 Page code (`Live Ops` page)
- On ready: `getLiveOpsBootstrap()` → hide/show role elements → seed embed via `postMessage({type:'amg-liveops-init', role, token, mapboxToken, supabaseUrl, self})`.
- Toggle handlers → `goActive`/`goInactive` → notify embed to start/stop tracker.
- First activation → open `consentLightbox`; store consent version.
- Listen for embed messages: `needs-token-refresh`, `perm-denied`, `resize`, `tracker-state`.
- `wixWindow.getCurrentGeolocation()` used once as a permission-priming fallback for browsers that block iframe geolocation (see risk R2).

---

## 7. Privacy, security & compliance

1. **Consent, versioned** — lightbox copy states what’s collected, who sees what (admins full / others first name), retention (72 h), and how to stop. Every grant/revoke → `location_consent_events` + `PortalAuditLogs`. Update Privacy Policy page.
2. **Server-side redaction only** — the public channel/payload is built server-side; no client-side hiding.
3. **Coordinate fuzzing for non-admins** (~1 km deterministic) — prevents clients/partners from inferring a crew member’s home address.
4. **Kill switches** — crew instant stop; admin force-deactivate; global feature flag in a `portal_settings` row.
5. **Auto-expiry** — 30-min stale timeout, 16-h duty cap, midnight-local sweep option.
6. **Retention** — pings 72 h (pg_cron), live status row zeroed on deactivate (`geom = null`), trails admin-only ≤ 24 h.
7. **Rate limiting & sanity** — per-crew ping throttle, accuracy gate, teleport rejection, payload schema validation in `.jsw`.
8. **Audit** — reuse `logAction()`: activate/deactivate, admin trail views, force-deactivations. Admin “trail viewed” events are themselves auditable (watch the watchers).
9. **Legal note (not legal advice):** if any crew are CA/EU residents, location is personal data — consent + purpose limitation + retention above are designed to satisfy CCPA/GDPR basics; have counsel review the consent copy.

---

## 8. Next-level enhancements (Phase 2+)

| Feature | What it does | Effort |
|---|---|---|
| **Mission overlay** | Plot live `missions` (dep/arr airports geocoded from `airports` ref table, great-circle route lines); assigned crew dots linked by leader lines; click mission → details | M |
| **Nearest-crew dispatch** | Admin drops a pin / picks an airport → PostGIS `ST_DWithin` + certs filter → ranked list “closest qualified crew”, one-click assign → writes `mission_crew_assignments` | M |
| **Airport geofence auto-status** | Within 3 km of assigned departure airport → auto “At airport ✈” status + notify dispatch | S |
| **History trails & replay** | Admin toggles 24 h trail per crew; time-scrubber replay of the whole map (from `crew_location_pings`) | M |
| **Alerts** | Crew on active mission goes stale > 10 min → notification (existing `notifications` + SMS via your Twilio) ; crew enters/leaves geofence | M |
| **Weather layer** | NEXRAD radar raster tiles (RainViewer/IEM, free) + METAR station overlay for admins | S–M |
| **ADS-B aircraft layer** | Show company/known tail numbers in flight via OpenSky/adsb.fi API — the map becomes crew + aircraft ops picture | M |
| **Duty timer** | Active session length displayed on admin card; warn at configurable duty limits | S |
| **Privacy zones** | Crew can define a home-radius where their dot freezes at zone edge even for admins | S |
| **PWA install** | Manifest + service worker on Live Ops page → “installed app” feel, better wake behavior | S |

### Phase 3 — true background tracking (chosen direction)
Browser tabs cannot track in the background (OS suspends them). Plan:
- **Capacitor companion app** (“AMG Crew”) wrapping the portal’s crew surface; `@capacitor/geolocation` + background-geolocation plugin for significant-change updates; pushes pings to the same `postLocation` contract via an API token flow; iOS “Always” location permission w/ App Store privacy declarations; push notifications for mission assignments as the carrot for installing.
- Web remains the universal fallback; `source` column already distinguishes `web` vs `companion_app`.
- Effort: ~3–4 wks incl. store review; scope separately after Phase 1 proves adoption.

---

## 9. Rollout plan

| Phase | Scope | Est. |
|---|---|---|
| **0. Foundations** | PostGIS migration, tables/RLS/cron, secrets in Wix, identity bridge (email↔profiles), tighten `permissions.json` | 2–3 d |
| **1a. Tracker + write path** | `locationTracking.jsw`, consent lightbox, Go Active on Crew Dashboard + Live Ops, embed tracker runtime | 3–4 d |
| **1b. Map viewer** | Mapbox embed, Realtime channels + JWT mint, role-shaped hover, stale states, clustering, roster panel | 4–5 d |
| **1c. Hardening + beta** | Rate limits, reconnect/degraded mode, mobile QA, 3–5 crew pilot beta, consent copy review | 2–3 d |
| **2. Ops-grade** | Mission overlay, nearest-crew dispatch, geofences, trails/replay, alerts, weather | 5–8 d |
| **3. Companion app** | Capacitor background tracking | separate |

## 10. Security flags found during review (fix in Phase 0)
1. `src/backend/permissions.json` currently allows **anonymous invoke of every web method**. In-function checks mitigate, but flip to `siteMember`-only.
2. **`cms/*.csv` contains crew PII (emails/names) and the repo is public.** Remove from the repo (git filter-repo to purge history), .gitignore `cms/`, and treat any real data in those files as exposed.
3. When adding secrets, never commit them — Wix Secrets Manager only; Mapbox token must be a URL-restricted *public* token.

## 11. Testing & QA
- **Unit (backend):** role gating per function, payload shaping (admin vs public), ping validation (accuracy/teleport/rate), consent state machine.
- **Integration:** two-browser test (crew phone + admin desktop) — latency, stale transition at 3 min, auto-deactivate at 30 min, WebSocket kill → polling fallback.
- **Privacy assertions:** network-capture test proving non-admin payloads contain only `{crew_id, first_name, fuzzed coords, state}`; RLS test that anon/authenticated keys can’t read tables directly.
- **Devices:** iOS Safari (permission quirks, backgrounding), Android Chrome, desktop; low-battery and denied-permission paths.
- **Load:** simulate 100 active crews + 25 viewers (script inserting pings via service key) — map fps, Realtime fan-out, wix-data quota headroom.

## 12. Cost (monthly, est.)
- **Mapbox:** free ≤ 50k loads; a 60-user portal ≈ well within free tier ($0).
- **Supabase:** existing project; Realtime messages + storage negligible at this scale ($0 incremental on paid plan).
- **Weather/ADS-B layers:** free tiers available. Net: ~$0 until significant scale.

## 13. Risks & mitigations
| Risk | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| R1 Browser tab suspends when phone locks (web can’t background-track) | Expectation-setting in UI (“sharing while portal open”), heartbeat + stale states, Phase 3 app |
| R2 Wix HTML embed iframe lacks geolocation `allow` attribute in some templates | Verify early spike (day 1); fallback = interval `wixWindow.getCurrentGeolocation()` from page code feeding embed |
| R3 Realtime JWT complexity | Fallback path already specced: 20 s role-shaped polling through `.jsw` |
| R4 Crew adoption | Make “Active” status visible to dispatch = more assignments (incentive), 1-tap toggle, no forms |
| R5 PII leak via public repo/service keys | §10 fixes, secrets manager, URL-restricted tokens, RLS deny-all |

## 14. Open questions
1. Should clients/partners see the map at all, or only admins + crew? (Plan assumes all roles per your spec — easy to restrict later via `role_permissions` module row `live_ops`.)
2. Which system is the long-term source of truth — Wix collections or the Supabase `profiles`/`crew_profiles` schema? (Plan bridges by email; a deliberate migration decision would simplify.)
3. Day rate on admin hover: include or omit? (Sensitive even for admins’ screens in public places — default: omit, show in roster panel only.)
4. Consent copy: legal review owner?

AMG Aviation Group

Production Next.js/Vercel website and integrated AMG portal system.

Included
Public AMG Aviation website
AMG Connect Operations Deck portal (v2 rebuild): Client, Crew, Admin/Operations, and Partner (broker/vendor) workspaces
Supabase Auth/profile-based role routing (client, crew, admin, partner, super_admin)
Aircraft management and client aircraft visibility
Mission requests, mission control board, crew assignment, partner service requests
Quotes, client quote review, invoices, payment-status tracking
Crew expense submission, admin review, and invoice linkage
Documents, messaging, notification center, audit log
Admin user creation, approvals, waitlist, and invitation workflow
Higgsfield-ready media placeholder registry
Portal Design System (Operations Deck)

The portal UI is a self-contained design system scoped under .amg-portal:

Tokens: --deck-* variables in app/globals.css (navy chrome, light canvas, champagne-gold accent), with shadcn and legacy --amg-* tokens re-pointed to the same palette
Component classes: .deck-card, .deck-inset, .deck-eyebrow, .deck-chip, .deck-input, .deck-nav-link, etc. (defined in @layer components)
Kit: components/portal/ui/* (PageHeader, SectionCard, StatCard, RecordRow, QuickLink, FilterTabs, Pagination, DataTable, StatusBadge, fields, NotificationsList)
Shell: components/portal/shell/portal-shell.tsx (dark sidebar with grouped nav per role, Zulu clock, notifications, admin view switcher)
Navigation IA: DECK_NAV in lib/portal/constants.ts

Note: a global rule zeroes tracking-* utilities; portal code uses [letter-spacing:...] arbitrary properties or the .deck-eyebrow classes instead.

Local Development
npm install
npm run dev

Open http://localhost:3000.

Validation
npm run lint
npm run typecheck
npm run stripe:verify
npm run stripe:subscriptions:verify
npm run stripe:live-readiness:verify
npm run test
npm run build
Stripe Invoice Payments

Invoice emails and portal invoice detail pages can create server-side Stripe Checkout Sessions for open invoices. Configure these variables locally and in Vercel:

STRIPE_SECRET_KEY (sk_test_... locally/preview, sk_live_... in Vercel Production)
STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET (matching the current Stripe mode webhook endpoint)
NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL or NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL
NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY only if a future client-side Stripe.js flow is added

Stripe dashboard setup:

Add the webhook endpoint https://YOUR_DOMAIN/api/webhooks/stripe.
Subscribe to checkout.session.completed, checkout.session.expired, payment_intent.succeeded, payment_intent.payment_failed, and payment_intent.canceled.
Enable the desired payment methods in Stripe Checkout.
Configure Stripe branding/logo/color to match AMG.
Test in Stripe test mode before production.

Local test flow:

Use Stripe test keys and run the app locally.
Forward webhooks with Stripe CLI to /api/webhooks/stripe.
Create and send a test invoice.
Click Pay Invoice and use a Stripe test card.
Confirm the webhook marks the invoice paid and sends one receipt email.
Stripe Subscription Billing

Stripe Billing is the source of truth for recurring AMG subscriptions. The portal stores a local mirror for operational/admin visibility and updates it from Stripe webhooks.

Required setup:

Add STRIPE_SECRET_KEY and STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET.
Create Stripe Products/Prices for AMG subscription plans and tiers.
Add test Price IDs to AMG plan tiers for local/preview and live Price IDs for production.
Configure webhook endpoint https://YOUR_DOMAIN/api/webhooks/stripe.
Subscribe to checkout.session.completed, checkout.session.expired, customer.created, customer.updated, customer.subscription.created, customer.subscription.updated, customer.subscription.deleted, invoice.created, invoice.finalized, invoice.paid, invoice.payment_failed, and payment_method.attached.
Configure Stripe Customer Portal if clients should manage payment methods or view invoices.
Configure Stripe branding and test in Stripe test mode before production.

Manual test flow:

Create a Stripe test product/price.
Add the test price ID to an AMG subscription tier.
Create a portal subscription setup link as an admin.
Complete Stripe Checkout with a test card.
Confirm webhook updates portal status to active or trialing.
Create or change a subscription directly in Stripe and verify it syncs or appears as needs_review.
Test payment failure/cancellation and confirm admin warnings/event history update.
Production Setup
Apply docs/AMG_PRODUCTION_DATABASE_PATCHES.sql in Supabase.
Configure environment variables from .env.local.example in Vercel.
Confirm private storage buckets exist for documents and crew-credentials.
Configure Supabase Auth email templates and redirect URL.
Add Resend/Twilio credentials when external delivery is ready.

Detailed setup lives in docs/DEPLOYMENT.md, docs/NOTIFICATIONS.md, and docs/BILLING.md.

Stripe Live Account Readiness

Production Stripe mode is inferred from STRIPE_SECRET_KEY: sk_test_... is test mode and sk_live_... is live mode. The app does not require STRIPE_MODE and never sends Stripe secret values to the browser.

Vercel Production must use:

STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=sk_live_...
STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET=whsec_... from the live webhook endpoint
NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL=https://www.amgaviationgroup.com
NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=pk_live_... only if client-side Stripe.js is used

Vercel Preview/local should use Stripe test keys unless AMG intentionally wants live billing there.

Subscription tiers support separate test and live Stripe Price IDs. Live mode blocks subscription checkout unless the selected tier/cadence has a live Price ID. Test mode uses a test Price ID, or the legacy Price ID fields for backwards-compatible local testing, and rejects live-only configuration.

Admins can review Stripe mode, configured-key presence, webhook status, missing Price ID counts, and last webhook status at /portal/admin/settings/billing. The page only displays yes/no status and counts; it never displays secret values.

---
/**
* Role-permission vocabulary: the module catalog, the action list, and the
* code-default matrix. Pure data — safe to import from client components,
* server code, and scripts (no "server-only", no Supabase).
*
* Resolution semantics live in lib/portal/permissions.ts: a role_permissions
* DB row wins, then DEFAULT_PERMISSIONS, then deny. super_admin always passes
* and is intentionally absent from this file's matrix.
*/

import type { PortalRole } from "@/lib/portal/constants";

export const PERMISSION_ACTIONS = ["view", "add", "edit", "delete"] as const;
export type PermissionAction = (typeof PERMISSION_ACTIONS)[number];

export const PERMISSION_ACTION_LABELS: Record<PermissionAction, string> = {
view: "View",
add: "Add",
edit: "Edit",
delete: "Delete",
};

export type PermissionModule =
| "missions"
| "quotes"
| "invoices"
| "payments"
| "subscriptions"
| "expenses"
| "documents"
| "messages"
| "communications"
| "notifications"
| "aircraft"
| "passengers"
| "clients"
| "crew"
| "partners"
| "users"
| "crm"
| "form_submissions"
| "network_applications"
| "tasks"
| "compliance"
| "audit_log"
| "financial_analytics"
| "settings";

export type PermissionKey = `${PermissionModule}.${PermissionAction}`;

/** Roles that appear as editable rows in the matrix. super_admin never does. */
export const MATRIX_ROLES = ["client", "crew", "partner", "admin"] as const;
export type MatrixRole = (typeof MATRIX_ROLES)[number];

export function isMatrixRole(value: unknown): value is MatrixRole {
return value === "client" || value === "crew" || value === "partner" || value === "admin";
}

export type PermissionModuleInfo = {
key: PermissionModule;
label: string;
description: string;
};

/** Every permission-gated business area of the portal, in display order. */
export const PERMISSION_MODULES: PermissionModuleInfo[] = [
{ key: "missions", label: "Missions & Support Requests", description: "Trips, mission control, calendar, mission lifecycle." },
{ key: "quotes", label: "Quotes", description: "Quote drafting, sending, and client responses." },
{ key: "invoices", label: "Invoices & Payments", description: "Invoices, payments, receivables." },
{ key: "payments", label: "Payments Authority", description: "Recording payments, voids, write-offs, refunds." },
{ key: "subscriptions", label: "Subscriptions", description: "Plans, member subscriptions, usage and credits." },
{ key: "expenses", label: "Expenses & Receipts", description: "Expense capture, review, and receipts." },
{ key: "documents", label: "Documents", description: "Document vault uploads, review, and sharing." },
{ key: "messages", label: "Messages", description: "Portal message threads." },
{ key: "communications", label: "Email Communications", description: "Communications center and templated email sends." },
{ key: "notifications", label: "Notifications", description: "In-app notification feed." },
{ key: "aircraft", label: "Aircraft", description: "Aircraft records and airworthiness data." },
{ key: "passengers", label: "Passengers", description: "Saved passenger profiles." },
{ key: "clients", label: "Client Directory", description: "Client company and contact records." },
{ key: "crew", label: "Crew Directory & Records", description: "Crew roster, availability, credentials." },
{ key: "partners", label: "Partners & Service Requests", description: "Partner directory, partner workspace, assigned requests." },
{ key: "users", label: "User Management", description: "Approvals, waitlist, roles, account admin." },
{ key: "crm", label: "Sales Pipeline (CRM)", description: "Leads, activities, follow-ups." },
{ key: "form_submissions", label: "Form Submissions", description: "Public website form intake." },
{ key: "network_applications", label: "Network Applications", description: "Crew network applications and review." },
{ key: "tasks", label: "Tasks", description: "Operational task list." },
{ key: "compliance", label: "Compliance & Legal", description: "Consent events, privacy requests, controls." },
{ key: "audit_log", label: "Audit Log", description: "Immutable audit trail (view only by design)." },
{ key: "financial_analytics", label: "Financial Analytics", description: "Revenue and margin reporting." },
{ key: "settings", label: "Portal Settings", description: "Billing settings, email templates, permissions." },
];

export const PERMISSION_MODULE_KEYS: PermissionModule[] = PERMISSION_MODULES.map((m) => m.key);

export function isPermissionModule(value: unknown): value is PermissionModule {
return PERMISSION_MODULE_KEYS.includes(value as PermissionModule);
}

export type ActionFlags = Record<PermissionAction, boolean>;

const NONE: ActionFlags = { view: false, add: false, edit: false, delete: false };
const V: ActionFlags = { view: true, add: false, edit: false, delete: false };
const VA: ActionFlags = { view: true, add: true, edit: false, delete: false };
const VE: ActionFlags = { view: true, add: false, edit: true, delete: false };
const VAE: ActionFlags = { view: true, add: true, edit: true, delete: false };
const FULL: ActionFlags = { view: true, add: true, edit: true, delete: true };

/**
* Code defaults = today's behavior, so shipping the matrix changes nothing
* until an admin edits it. A module absent for a role means deny-all; it is
* filled in explicitly here so the seed and the matrix UI show every cell.
* Ownership scoping (own records only) stays inside the actions + RLS.
*/
export const DEFAULT_PERMISSIONS: Record<MatrixRole, Record<PermissionModule, ActionFlags>> = {
client: {
missions: VAE,
quotes: VE,
invoices: V,
payments: NONE,
subscriptions: VAE,
expenses: NONE,
documents: VA,
messages: VA,
communications: NONE,
notifications: VE,
aircraft: VAE,
passengers: FULL,
clients: NONE,
crew: NONE,
partners: NONE,
users: NONE,
crm: NONE,
form_submissions: NONE,
network_applications: NONE,
tasks: NONE,
compliance: NONE,
audit_log: NONE,
financial_analytics: NONE,
settings: NONE,
},
crew: {
missions: VE,
quotes: NONE,
invoices: NONE,
payments: NONE,
subscriptions: NONE,
expenses: FULL,
documents: VA,
messages: VA,
communications: NONE,
notifications: VE,
aircraft: NONE,
passengers: NONE,
clients: NONE,
// view/add/edit own records; hard-deleting credential/compliance
// artifacts stays admin-only (availability removal is wired as edit)
crew: VAE,
partners: NONE,
users: NONE,
crm: NONE,
form_submissions: NONE,
network_applications: NONE,
tasks: NONE,
compliance: NONE,
audit_log: NONE,
financial_analytics: NONE,
settings: NONE,
},
partner: {
missions: NONE,
quotes: NONE,
invoices: NONE,
payments: NONE,
subscriptions: NONE,
expenses: NONE,
documents: VA,
messages: VA,
communications: NONE,
notifications: VE,
aircraft: NONE,
passengers: NONE,
clients: NONE,
crew: NONE,
partners: VAE,
users: NONE,
crm: NONE,
form_submissions: NONE,
network_applications: NONE,
tasks: NONE,
compliance: NONE,
audit_log: NONE,
financial_analytics: NONE,
settings: NONE,
},
admin: {
missions: FULL,
quotes: FULL,
invoices: FULL,
payments: FULL,
subscriptions: FULL,
expenses: FULL,
documents: FULL,
messages: FULL,
communications: FULL,
notifications: FULL,
aircraft: FULL,
passengers: FULL,
clients: FULL,
crew: FULL,
partners: FULL,
users: FULL,
crm: FULL,
form_submissions: FULL,
network_applications: FULL,
tasks: FULL,
compliance: FULL,
audit_log: V,
financial_analytics: V,
settings: FULL,
},
};

export function defaultFlags(role: PortalRole, module: PermissionModule): ActionFlags {
if (role === "super_admin") return FULL;
return DEFAULT_PERMISSIONS[role as MatrixRole]?.[module] ?? NONE;
}

export function parsePermissionKey(key: PermissionKey): { module: PermissionModule; action: PermissionAction } {
const dot = key.lastIndexOf(".");
if (dot < 0) {
// Unreachable for literal PermissionKeys; guards a future dynamic key.
throw new Error(`Invalid permission key: ${key}`);
}
return {
module: key.slice(0, dot) as PermissionModule,
action: key.slice(dot + 1) as PermissionAction,
};
}

/**
* Portal nav → module mapping by route prefix (longest prefix wins). Routes
* with no entry (dashboards, personal settings, sign-out) are always shown.
*/
export const NAV_MODULE_PREFIXES: [string, PermissionModule][] = [
["/portal/admin/mission-control", "missions"],
["/portal/admin/trips", "missions"],
["/portal/admin/calendar", "missions"],
["/portal/admin/missions", "missions"],
["/portal/admin/tasks", "tasks"],
["/portal/admin/crm", "crm"],
["/portal/admin/form-submissions", "form_submissions"],
["/portal/admin/network-applications", "network_applications"],
["/portal/admin/user-approvals", "users"],
["/portal/admin/waitlist", "users"],
["/portal/admin/users", "users"],
["/portal/admin/clients", "clients"],
["/portal/admin/crew", "crew"],
["/portal/admin/aircraft", "aircraft"],
["/portal/admin/partners", "partners"],
["/portal/admin/quotes", "quotes"],
["/portal/admin/invoices", "invoices"],
["/portal/admin/payments", "payments"],
["/portal/admin/receivables", "invoices"],
["/portal/admin/subscriptions", "subscriptions"],
// Longest-prefix-wins: the pricing catalog rides "settings" while the rest
// of /portal/admin/financial stays under "financial_analytics".
["/portal/admin/financial/pricing", "settings"],
["/portal/admin/financial", "financial_analytics"],
["/portal/admin/expenses", "expenses"],
["/portal/admin/receipts", "expenses"],
["/portal/admin/messages", "messages"],
["/portal/admin/communications", "communications"],
["/portal/admin/notifications", "notifications"],
["/portal/admin/documents", "documents"],
["/portal/admin/compliance", "compliance"],
["/portal/admin/security-review", "compliance"],
["/portal/admin/audit-log", "audit_log"],
["/portal/admin/system-health", "settings"],
["/portal/admin/settings", "settings"],
["/portal/client/trips", "missions"],
["/portal/client/aircraft", "aircraft"],
["/portal/client/passengers", "passengers"],
["/portal/client/quotes", "quotes"],
["/portal/client/billing", "invoices"],
["/portal/client/subscriptions", "subscriptions"],
["/portal/client/documents", "documents"],

["/portal/client/notifications", "notifications"],

["/portal/partner/profile", "partners"],
["/portal/partner/documents", "documents"],
["/portal/partner/messages", "messages"],
["/portal/partner/notifications", "notifications"],
];

export function navModuleForHref(href: string): PermissionModule | null {
const path = href.split("?")[0];
let best: PermissionModule | null = null;

* Role-permission vocabulary: the module catalog, the action list, and the
* code-default matrix. Pure data — safe to import from client components,
* server code, and scripts (no "server-only", no Supabase).
*
* Resolution semantics live in lib/portal/permissions.ts: a role_permissions
* DB row wins, then DEFAULT_PERMISSIONS, then deny. super_admin always passes
* and is intentionally absent from this file's matrix.
*/

export const PERMISSION_ACTIONS = ["view", "add", "edit", "delete"] as const;

/** Roles that appear as editable rows in the matrix. super_admin never does. */
export const MATRIX_ROLES = ["client", "crew", "partner", "admin"] as const;

export function isMatrixRole(value: unknown): value is MatrixRole {
return value === "client" || value === "crew" || value === "partner" || value === "admin";
}

export type PermissionModuleInfo = {

if (role === "super_admin") return FULL;

export function parsePermissionKey(key: PermissionKey): { module: PermissionModule; action: PermissionAction } {
const dot = key.lastIndexOf(".");
if (dot < 0) {
// Unreachable for literal PermissionKeys; guards a future dynamic key.
throw new Error(`Invalid permission key: ${key}`);
}
return {
module: key.slice(0, dot) as PermissionModule,
action: key.slice(dot + 1) as PermissionAction,
};
}

# AMG Connect — Live Crew Map ("Live Ops")
## Implementation Plan v2.0 — July 7, 2026
### Target: `amgaviation/amg1` (Next.js 16 / Vercel / Supabase / Stripe portal)

> v2 replaces the earlier Wix-based plan. It is grounded in the current `main` branch:
> App Router portal under `app/portal/{admin,crew,client,partner}`, Supabase Auth +
> `profiles`-based role routing, the `role_permissions` matrix, server-action `actor()`
> guards, the Operations Deck design system, and your live Supabase project
> `AMG Aviation Portal` (vsynqnqlouvphiniqaiy).

---

## 1. Executive summary

A **Live Ops** map surface in the portal, available to every role, showing active crews in real time. Crew members tap **Go Active**, approve browser location permission, and appear as a live dot. **Admins/super-admins get the full intel card on hover** (name, phone, duty status, mission, certs, last ping). **Every other role sees the first name only** — enforced in the database, not the client. Built with: a `live_ops` permission module (so you can retune visibility per role from the existing admin settings matrix without a deploy), PostGIS + Supabase Realtime broadcast, and Mapbox GL JS in a client component inside the Deck shell.

Phasing:
- **Phase 0 (foundations, 2–3 d):** migrations (PostGIS, tables, RLS, broadcast triggers, cron), `live_ops` module registration, type regen.
- **Phase 1 (MVP, ~1.5 wks):** Go Active flow + consent, live map for all roles, role-shaped hover, stale/auto-expire handling, admin roster panel.
- **Phase 2 (ops-grade, ~1–2 wks):** mission overlay, nearest-crew dispatch, geofence auto-status, trails/replay, alerts, weather layer.
- **Phase 3 (background tracking):** Capacitor companion app for true background location (chosen direction; scoped separately).

---

## 2. Grounding — what this plan builds on (verified in repo)

| Existing asset | How Live Ops uses it |
|---|---|
| `lib/portal/session.ts` (`getSessionUser`, `requireUser`, `requireRole`) | Page + action auth; React-cached per request |
| `lib/portal/permissions.ts` + `permissions-catalog.ts` | New `live_ops` module → per-role view flags editable in admin settings; `requireRolePermission` page guard; `NAV_MODULE_PREFIXES` nav filtering |
| `app/portal/actions/_helpers.ts` `actor(roles, perm)` | Guard for `goActive`/`goInactive`/admin actions |
| `lib/supabase/client.ts` (browser) / `server.ts` (SSR + service) | Browser client already carries the user's Supabase Auth JWT → RLS + Realtime auth work natively, no token plumbing |
| `profiles` (role, status, home_base) + `crew_profiles` (first_name, certs, ratings, home_airport…) | Identity + hover-card content; no identity bridge needed (portal is already Supabase-native) |
| `missions`, `mission_crew_assignments`, `crew_availability` | Phase 2 overlays and duty context |
| `audit_events` via `lib/portal/audit.ts`; `consent_events` via `lib/compliance/consent.ts` | Activation/deactivation/consent/audit trail |
| `notifications` + `lib/portal/notification-delivery.ts` | Stale-crew and geofence alerts |
| `supabase/migrations/2026MMDDHHMMSS_*.sql` convention; `lib/supabase/database.types.ts` | Schema changes + `supabase gen types` regen |
| `scripts/verify-*.ts` + `npm run typecheck` | Add `scripts/verify-live-ops.ts` verification gate |
| Deck kit (`components/portal/ui/*`, `portal-shell.tsx`, `DECK_NAV`, `--deck-*` tokens) | Map page chrome, roster panel, status chips |
| `lib/crew-location-seeds.ts` + three.js globe (public site) | Marketing-globe concept graduates into the real ops map; seeds reusable for demo mode |
| Supabase project: PostGIS **available**, pg_cron **installed**, Realtime enabled | Phase 0 turns these on for location data |

No map library exists in `package.json` yet (three.js only). New deps: `mapbox-gl` (+ `@types/mapbox-gl`). Env: `NEXT_PUBLIC_MAPBOX_TOKEN` (URL-restricted public token).

---

## 3. Requirements

### 3.1 User stories
| # | As a… | I want… | So that… |
|---|---|---|---|
| U1 | Crew | to toggle Go Active and share my location | dispatch knows I'm available and where |
| U2 | Crew | to stop instantly, with sharing auto-expiring | I stay in control of my privacy |
| U3 | Admin | full crew intel on hover + roster panel | assign the closest qualified crew fast |
| U4 | Admin | stale/offline states visually distinct | I never act on dead data |
| U5 | Any user | first-name-only dots | situational awareness without PII exposure |
| U6 | Admin | live missions overlaid | one screen for the whole operation |
| U7 | Compliance | consent + audit + auto-purge | privacy obligations met |

### 3.2 Visibility matrix (enforced in Postgres — see §5.4)
| Data | admin / super_admin | crew | client | partner |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| First name | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Full name, phone, avatar | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Precise coords | ✅ | ❌ fuzzed ~1 km | ❌ fuzzed | ❌ fuzzed |
| Duty status, mission ref, base airport, certs/ratings | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Speed/heading, exact last-ping time | ✅ | ❌ (coarse Active/Stale only) | ❌ | ❌ |
| 24 h trail / replay | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |

Whether client/partner can open the tab at all = `live_ops.view` flag in the admin permissions matrix (default: on for all roles per your spec; one click to restrict later).

### 3.3 Non-functional
- Dot latency ≤ 2 s (Realtime push); polling fallback ≤ 20 s.
- Battery-sane: adaptive 15 s (moving) / 60 s (stationary) pings, high-accuracy GPS only while moving.
- Stale at 3 min (amber) → auto-deactivate at 30 min → hard cap 16 h.
- 200+ dots at 60 fps (clustering beyond ~50); mobile Safari/Chrome first-class.
- Zero Vercel invocations on the ping hot path (direct browser→Supabase writes under RLS; serverless only for activate/deactivate/admin actions).

---

## 4. Architecture

```
Crew browser (portal tab / installed PWA)
│  LiveOpsTracker (client component)
│   • navigator.geolocation.watchPosition (adaptive cadence, accuracy gate)
│   • supabase.from('crew_location_pings').insert(...)  ── RLS: auth.uid() = crew_id
│                                                           + is_active + rate/teleport
│                                                           checks in BEFORE trigger
▼
Supabase Postgres (PostGIS)
│  trigger on crew_location_pings ──► upsert crew_live_status
│  trigger on crew_live_status  ──►  realtime.broadcast_changes:
│       'crew-map:admin'   full payload      (private channel)
│       'crew-map:public'  {crew_id, first_name, fuzzed lat/lng, state}
│  realtime.messages RLS: :admin requires profiles.role ∈ (admin, super_admin);
│                         :public requires any approved portal role + live_ops.view
▼
Every viewer (same page, all roles)
│  LiveOpsMap (client component, Mapbox GL JS)
│   • initial load via RPC get_live_crew() (SECURITY DEFINER, role-shaped rows)
│   • subscribes to the one channel its role authorizes
▼
Next.js server (only for control plane)
   server actions: goActive / goInactive / adminForceDeactivate / adminGetTrail
   guarded by actor(['crew'], 'live_ops.view') etc.; audit + consent writes
```

Why this shape:
- **The portal is already Supabase-native with Auth** — the browser client's JWT drives RLS and Realtime channel authorization directly. No token minting, no iframe bridges.
- **Ping hot path skips Vercel.** 20 active crews at 15 s cadence ≈ 115k writes/day — trivial for Postgres, expensive as serverless invocations. Validation lives in a `BEFORE INSERT` trigger (rate limit, accuracy ≤ 150 m, teleport > 300 kt rejected, `is_active` required).
- **Redaction in the database.** Non-admins are never granted SELECT on the live tables; they can only receive the public broadcast/RPC, which contains first name + fuzzed coords and nothing else. View-source and the network tab reveal nothing.
- **Mapbox GL JS** per your choice (free ≤ 50k loads/mo; MapLibre GL is a drop-in zero-cost fallback if you ever want off Mapbox).

---

## 5. Database (Supabase migrations, following `2026MMDDHHMMSS_*.sql`)

### 5.1 `20260707120000_live_ops_foundations.sql`
```sql
create extension if not exists postgis;

create table public.crew_live_status (
  crew_id        uuid primary key references public.profiles(id) on delete cascade,
  is_active      boolean not null default false,
  activated_at   timestamptz,
  deactivated_at timestamptz,
  deactivation_reason text check (deactivation_reason in ('manual','timeout','duty_cap','admin')),
  last_ping_at   timestamptz,
  geom           geography(point,4326),
  accuracy_m     double precision,
  speed_mps      double precision,
  heading_deg    double precision,
  battery_pct    int,
  activity_state text not null default 'stationary' check (activity_state in ('moving','stationary')),
  status_note    text,                                   -- crew-entered: "At FBO, wheels up 1400Z"
  duty_status    text not null default 'available' check (duty_status in ('available','on_mission','resting')),
  mission_id     uuid references public.missions(id) on delete set null,
  consent_version text,
  source         text not null default 'web' check (source in ('web','companion_app')),
  updated_at     timestamptz not null default now()
);
create index crew_live_status_geom_idx on public.crew_live_status using gist (geom);
create index crew_live_status_active_idx on public.crew_live_status (is_active, last_ping_at);

create table public.crew_location_pings (
  id bigint generated always as identity,
  crew_id uuid not null references public.profiles(id) on delete cascade,
  ping_at timestamptz not null default now(),
  geom geography(point,4326) not null,
  accuracy_m double precision, speed_mps double precision, heading_deg double precision,
  battery_pct int, source text not null default 'web',
  primary key (id, ping_at)
) partition by range (ping_at);
-- pg_partman or 7 rolling daily partitions created by cron (below)
create index crew_location_pings_crew_idx on public.crew_location_pings (crew_id, ping_at desc);
```

### 5.2 Ping-path triggers (same migration)
- `BEFORE INSERT on crew_location_pings`: reject if no active `crew_live_status` row; enforce ≥ 5 s since `last_ping_at`; reject `accuracy_m > 150`; reject implied speed > 155 m/s (~300 kt) vs previous point; clamp battery/heading ranges.
- `AFTER INSERT`: upsert `crew_live_status` (geom, last_ping_at, speed, heading, `activity_state = speed > 1 ? 'moving' : 'stationary'`).

### 5.3 Realtime broadcast (same migration)
`AFTER UPDATE on crew_live_status` trigger calls `realtime.broadcast_changes` twice:
- topic `crew-map:admin` — full row + joined `first_name`/`full_name` snapshot.
- topic `crew-map:public` — `{crew_id, first_name, lat, lng, state}` where lat/lng are **deterministically fuzzed ~1 km** (hash of crew_id + date-hour as offset seed, so dots don't jitter yet can't be averaged back) and `state ∈ (active, stale, off)` only.

`realtime.messages` RLS policies:
```sql
-- :admin — admins only
create policy live_admin_channel on realtime.messages for select to authenticated
  using (realtime.topic() = 'crew-map:admin'
     and exists (select 1 from public.profiles p
                 where p.id = auth.uid() and p.role in ('admin','super_admin') and p.status='approved'));
-- :public — any approved portal user
create policy live_public_channel on realtime.messages for select to authenticated
  using (realtime.topic() = 'crew-map:public'
     and exists (select 1 from public.profiles p
                 where p.id = auth.uid() and p.status='approved'));
```

### 5.4 Table RLS (deny-by-default, same migration)
- `crew_live_status`: crew can `select/update` **their own row only** (toggle + status note); admins `select` all (their postgres_changes/trail features); no client/partner access at all.
- `crew_location_pings`: crew `insert` own (`crew_id = auth.uid()`); admins `select`; nobody else anything.
- Initial map load via RPCs (`security definer`, role check inside):
  - `get_live_crew_admin()` → full rows (raises unless caller is admin/super_admin),
  - `get_live_crew_public()` → redacted+fuzzed rows (any approved role with `live_ops.view`).

### 5.5 Retention & sweeps — `20260707120100_live_ops_cron.sql` (pg_cron already installed)
- every minute: mark-stale is implicit (computed from `last_ping_at`), but auto-deactivate rows stale > 30 min (`deactivation_reason='timeout'`) and broadcast the change;
- hourly: drop ping partitions older than **72 h**;
- daily: enforce 16 h duty cap (`duty_cap`).

### 5.6 Permission module seed — `20260707120200_live_ops_permission_module.sql`
Insert `live_ops` rows into `role_permissions` for client/crew/partner/admin (view-only for non-admins, FULL for admin) so the existing admin settings matrix can manage access immediately.

### 5.7 Types
`supabase gen types typescript` → refresh `lib/supabase/database.types.ts` (repo convention).

## 6. Application changes (Next.js)

### 6.1 Permission catalog (`lib/portal/permissions-catalog.ts`)
- Add `"live_ops"` to `PermissionModule` union + `PERMISSION_MODULES` (label: "Live Ops Map", description: "Real-time crew location map and sharing controls.").
- `DEFAULT_PERMISSIONS`: client `V`, crew `VE` (view + edit = manage own sharing), partner `V`, admin `FULL`.
- `NAV_MODULE_PREFIXES`: add `["/portal/admin/live-ops","live_ops"]`, `["/portal/crew/live-ops","live_ops"]`, `["/portal/client/live-ops","live_ops"]`, `["/portal/partner/live-ops","live_ops"]`.

### 6.2 Routes (per-role shells, shared core)

```
app/portal/{admin|crew|client|partner}/live-ops/page.tsx   // thin: requireRolePermission([role],'live_ops') → <LiveOpsView role=… self=…/>
components/portal/live-ops/
  live-ops-view.tsx        // layout: map + (admin roster panel | crew sharing card)
  live-ops-map.tsx         // 'use client' — Mapbox GL, realtime subscription, hover cards
  live-ops-tracker.tsx     // 'use client' — geolocation watcher (crew only, mounted when active)
  go-active-card.tsx       // toggle, consent gate, status note, session timer (Deck kit styling)
  admin-roster-panel.tsx   // active/stale list, search, locate→flyTo, force-deactivate, trail toggle
  hover-card.tsx           // admin full card / minimal first-name chip
lib/portal/live-ops.ts     // types, payload parsing, fuzz/stale helpers shared client+server
app/portal/actions/live-ops.ts  // server actions (below)
```
`DECK_NAV` (`lib/portal/constants.ts`): add "Live Ops" item (lucide `radar`/`map-pin` icon) in each role's group; existing `navModuleForHref` filtering hides it when an admin turns a role's `live_ops.view` off.

### 6.3 Server actions (`app/portal/actions/live-ops.ts`, `actor()` pattern)
```ts
goActive(formData)            // actor(['crew'],'live_ops.view') → consent check (lib/compliance/consent.ts,
                              // consent type 'location_sharing' vN) → service upsert crew_live_status
                              // is_active=true → audit_events('live_ops.activated')
goInactive(reason)            // crew self-stop → audit
setStatusNote(note)           // ≤120 chars, sanitized
adminForceDeactivate(crewId)  // actor(undefined,'live_ops.delete') → audit('live_ops.force_deactivated')
adminGetCrewTrail(crewId, h)  // actor(undefined,'live_ops.edit') → ≤24h pings, downsampled;
                              // logs audit('live_ops.trail_viewed') — watch the watchers
```
Pings intentionally bypass server actions (direct RLS insert, §4).

### 6.4 `LiveOpsTracker` (crew browser)
- `watchPosition` with `enableHighAccuracy` only when `activity_state==='moving'`; throttle to 15 s moving / 60 s stationary; skip fixes < 10 m apart while stationary (heartbeat still ~60 s so staleness is meaningful).
- Client-side accuracy gate (>150 m dropped) mirrors the DB trigger; Battery API when available.
- `visibilitychange`: hidden > 5 min → pause + UI note ("sharing pauses while tab hidden — keep the portal open"); resume on visible. Honest copy about web limits until the Phase 3 app.
- Permission-denied → inline Deck-styled guidance per browser; `sonner` toasts for state changes.
- Unload (`pagehide`) → best-effort `navigator.sendBeacon` to a tiny route handler marking `deactivation_reason='timeout'` fast-path.

### 6.5 `LiveOpsMap` (all roles)
- Mapbox GL JS dark style (`dark-v11` variant matching Deck navy/champagne tokens; AMG-branded style later).
- Initial data: role-appropriate RPC; live updates: the one authorized broadcast channel; on WS drop → exponential backoff → 20 s RPC polling + degraded-mode banner.
- Rendering: GeoJSON source; circle layer with state colors (green active / amber stale / gray fading 10 min post-off); pulsing halo on fresh ping; heading chevron when moving; cluster layer > 50 dots; `flyTo` from roster.
- Hover (desktop) / tap (mobile):
  - **Admin card:** avatar/initials, full name, phone (tel: link), duty chip, mission ref → `/portal/admin/missions/[id]`, base airport, cert/type-rating chips (from `crew_profiles`), speed + "updated 12 s ago", actions: Locate · Trail · Message (`/portal/admin/messages`) · Force-deactivate.
  - **Everyone else:** chip: `● Tony — Active`. The payload contains nothing more to show.
- Controls: fit-all, fullscreen, status filter chips, legend; admin search box (name/airport).
- Empty state: "No crews active right now" + (crew) nudge to Go Active.

### 6.6 Crew dashboard touchpoint
`app/portal/crew/dashboard`: compact "Location Sharing" `SectionCard` (toggle + status + link to Live Ops). Admin dashboard: `StatCard` "Crews active now" linking to admin Live Ops.

## 7. Privacy, security, compliance
1. **Versioned consent** before first activation — reuse `lib/compliance/consent.ts` + `consent_events` (`location_sharing` v1): what's collected, who sees what, 72 h retention, how to stop. Update privacy policy page (`lib/compliance/legal-pages.ts`).
2. **DB-enforced redaction + fuzzing** (§5.3–5.4) — non-admins physically cannot receive PII or precise coords.
3. **Kill switches** — crew instant stop; admin force-deactivate; `live_ops.view` flags per role in settings matrix act as a feature gate.
4. **Auto-expiry** — 30 min stale timeout, 16 h duty cap, tab-hidden pause.
5. **Retention** — pings 72 h (partition drops); `crew_live_status.geom` nulled on deactivate; trails admin-only ≤ 24 h.
6. **Abuse resistance** — DB rate limit + teleport rejection; RLS `auth.uid()` binding prevents spoofing other crews.
7. **Audit everything** — activate/deactivate/force/trail-view → `audit_events` (existing audit-log UI picks them up).
8. **Legal note (not legal advice):** location = personal data (GDPR/CCPA if applicable crews); consent + purpose limitation + retention above are designed for that; have counsel review consent copy.

### Phase 3 — background tracking (your chosen direction)
Browsers cannot track with the screen off. Ship a **Capacitor "AMG Crew" companion app** wrapping the crew portal surface: background-geolocation plugin (significant-change mode), pings into the same `crew_location_pings` contract (`source='companion_app'`) using Supabase session auth; push notifications for mission assignments as the install incentive; iOS "Always" permission + store privacy declarations. ~3–4 wks incl. review; scope after Phase 1 proves adoption.

## 10. Testing (repo conventions)
- `scripts/verify-live-ops.ts` (tsx, like `verify-stripe-*.ts`): asserts RLS matrix (crew can't read others' rows; client/partner can't read tables at all; public RPC returns exactly 5 fields; fuzz ≥ 300 m from true point; ping trigger rejects fast/inaccurate/teleporting fixes), permission-module registration, cron jobs present.
- `npm run typecheck` gate (their `test` script).
- Manual: two-browser test (crew phone + admin desktop) — latency, 3 min stale transition, 30 min auto-off, WS kill → polling fallback, permission-denied paths, iOS Safari backgrounding.
- Network-capture privacy assertion: non-admin session's WS frames + RPC responses contain only `{crew_id, first_name, lat, lng, state}`.
- Load: seed script inserts 150 synthetic active crews (reuse `lib/crew-location-seeds.ts` airports) — map fps, broadcast fan-out, cluster behavior.

## 11. Cost
Mapbox free tier (≤ 50k loads/mo) ≈ $0 at current portal size; Supabase Realtime/storage negligible; ping path adds no Vercel invocation cost by design. Net ~$0/mo until major scale.

## 12. Risks
| Risk | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| Web can't background-track (screen off = no pings) | Honest UI copy, heartbeat/stale design, Phase 3 app |
| Realtime broadcast-from-DB is newer Supabase surface | Fallback specced: 20 s RPC polling (same payload shapers) |
| iOS Safari geolocation quirks (permission per-site, PWA differences) | Device QA in 1c; permission-recovery UX |
| Ping-write RLS abuse (scripted spam) | DB rate limit trigger + accuracy/teleport gates + partition-drop retention |
| Adoption | Active status drives assignments (visible incentive), 1-tap toggle, dashboard nudge |

Plan v2 is rewritten against the actual repo and saved to `docs/crew-live-map-plan.md` (in your connected folder — copy it into the Next.js repo when you're ready).

What changed now that it's grounded in the real stack, and why it's better than the Wix version:

**It plugs into systems you already built.** Access is a new `live_ops` module in your `permissions-catalog.ts` + `role_permissions` matrix — so who can see the map becomes editable from your existing admin settings screen, no redeploy. Pages guard with `requireRolePermission`, mutations use your `actor()` helper, consent reuses `lib/compliance/consent.ts`, audit flows into `audit_events`, and alerts ride your `notifications` delivery lib.

**The admin-vs-first-name rule is enforced in Postgres, not React.** Two Realtime broadcast channels (`crew-map:admin` / `crew-map:public`) gated by RLS on `realtime.messages` checking `profiles.role`; non-admins receive only `{crew_id, first_name, fuzzed coords, state}` — the PII never leaves the database for them. Coordinates are fuzzed ~1 km deterministically so clients can't infer a crew member's home.

**The ping hot path costs zero Vercel invocations.** Crew browsers write pings directly to Supabase under RLS (`auth.uid() = crew_id`), with rate-limit/accuracy/teleport validation in a DB trigger — ~115k pings/day would be wasteful as serverless calls.

One expectation to hold onto: a browser tab can't track with the screen off — that's physics of the web, which is why the plan's Phase 3 is the Capacitor companion app you opted for, sharing the same ping contract. Section 13 has five open questions; the first (whether clients/partners see the map on day one) is the only one that affects Phase 1 build order.
